name: Solana MCP Server
what_it_does: Solana MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with the Solana blockchain — querying account balances, fetching transaction history, reading on-chain program data, monitoring token accounts, accessing DeFi protocol state, and integrating Solana blockchain data into agent-driven crypto analytics, portfolio management, and Web3 development workflows.
use_cases: Querying Solana account balances and transaction history from crypto portfolio agents
Reading on-chain DeFi protocol state (liquidity pools, prices) from trading agents
Monitoring NFT holdings and collection data from Web3 agents
Tracking Solana validator performance and staking rewards from staking agents
Analyzing token flows and whale activity from blockchain analytics agents
Building Solana dApp development tools from Web3 developer agents
not_for: Ethereum or other EVM chains (Solana-specific; use Ethereum MCP for EVM)
Executing transactions without explicit user confirmation (read-heavy recommended)
best_when: An agent needs to query Solana blockchain data for analytics, monitoring, or Web3 development — read-only access to on-chain state for research and portfolio tracking.
avoid_when: You need Ethereum/EVM blockchain data, or need to execute transactions at high frequency — use appropriate chain-specific MCPs.

auth.notes: Solana RPC endpoint required — public endpoints (Mainnet, Devnet) available for free. Helius, QuickNode, or Alchemy API keys recommended for production use to avoid rate limits.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Public Solana RPC: free with aggressive rate limits; provider plans for reliability
pricing.paid_tiers: RPC provider plans (Helius, QuickNode, Alchemy) for production use
pricing.notes: Public Solana RPC is free but heavily rate-limited. Production use requires an RPC provider. MCP server is free.

performance.rate_limits: Public RPC: 100 requests/10s; provider plans for higher limits

known_agent_gotchas: CRITICAL: Transaction signing capability could lead to real fund loss — require explicit confirmation
Public RPC rate limits are severe — implement exponential backoff and use dedicated RPC provider
Solana mainnet vs devnet/testnet must be explicitly configured — wrong endpoint = wrong data
Account data requires Borsh deserialization for program-specific formats — complex for custom programs
Community MCP — Solana ecosystem evolves rapidly; API coverage may not include newest features
RPC provider API keys should be secured — exposing them risks quota theft
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. CRITICAL: If transaction signing enabled, real funds at risk. Read-only recommended for agents. Secure RPC provider API key. Community MCP.

security_score_components.security_notes: CRITICAL: Transaction signing = real fund risk. Read-only recommended. Secure RPC key. Community MCP.
